message:
I am thinking...: 
The synthesis log is still at 5713 lines and showing item 83/176 `$19\d_invalid_32bit[0:0]`. This has been the same for quite a while now. The time now is around 10:14 (when I last checked), and synthesis has been going for about 6-7 minutes.

Wait - looking at the yosys elapsed time: "5:40" at 10:14. The run started at 10:07 (around the time of the second attempt). So it's been ~7 minutes. That's actually quite early. The log might just not be getting flushed to disk frequently by Yosys.

The log file size isn't changing but the CPU usage is still showing activity (8.7% CPU). The process is alive. Yosys is probably in the middle of computing the decoder for item 83 which is the hazard3_decode module with 176 signals - this is a very large decode operation.

I should just wait and be patient. The big Hazard3 RISC-V CPU with all the peripherals will take significant synthesis time. Let me wait more.

Actually - looking again at the synthesis log tail, items 81-83 are being processed. The log shows 83 items have been logged, meaning Yosys is actively progressing through the decoder creation. The 83rd item corresponds to `$19\d_invalid_32bit` which counts backward from 176 (so 176-83+1 = ~93rd actual signal in the decoder). It seems to be making progress but the log isn't being written fast enough for me to observe changes in short polling intervals.

Let me try a much longer wait (1+ hour).
